title: Xtra Crispy British Cheese Ploughmans Pie
description: A garlicky, flaky, kid-friendly British pie infused with matcha and topped with spherified cheese bursts, perfect for a playful lunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and line a 9x13 inch baking pan with parchment paper.
In a bowl combine shredded cheddar, grated parmesan, matcha powder, and garlic powder; set aside.
In a saucepan heat heavy cream until steaming, then stir in the cheese mixture until melted and smooth.
In a separate bowl whisk flour and butter until crumbly, then stir in egg and salt and form a dough.
Roll the dough into a circle, place on the prepared pan, and press to line the bottom and up the sides.
Pour the warm cheese filling over the crust and spread evenly.
Bake for 25 minutes until the top is golden and the filling is set.
While the pie is baking, dissolve 5g sodium alginate in 150ml water, whisking until no lumps remain.
Cool the cheese filling to room temperature, then stir in the alginate solution to create a matcha cheese base for spherification.
Prepare a calcium lactate bath by dissolving 5g calcium lactate in 150ml water.
Drop spoonfuls of the matcha cheese mixture into the calcium bath to form spheres; let them sit for 2 minutes before removing with a slotted spoon.
Garnish the finished pie with a few matcha cheese spheres before serving.
